codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly rejects memories with cosine distance < 0.15 to prevent duplication. The feature adds SimilarMemoryFound error variant, cosine_distance helper, find_similar_memory method (repository-scoped), and modifies store() to reject similar entries. All 8 test cases pass covering rejection, acceptance, no-embedding, repository isolation, and edge cases.

issues.description: Performance: find_similar_memory fetches ALL entries with embeddings in the repository and iterates in Rust. No partial index on (repository, embedding IS NOT NULL) exists. For large repositories this could be slow, but acceptable for MVP.

Documentation inconsistency in expectedBehavior states 'Reject if cosine distance < 0.85' but the feature description says 'cosine distance 0.15'. Implementation correctly uses 0.15 per the main description. This is a doc issue, not code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed all Rust conventions correctly: async_trait for async trait methods, thiserror for errors, proper error propagation with ?, comprehensive test coverage in #[cfg(test)] module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 211 swell-memory tests pass, clippy reports no warnings, format check passes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill guidelines properly. No procedure deviations noted in skillFeedback.
Worker used standard cargo commands (build, test, fmt, clippy) which are all documented in AGENTS.md commands section. No undocumented services started.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands run: cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-memory -- similarity, cargo fmt -- --check, cargo clippy -p swell-memory

summary: mem-similarity-check passes scrutiny. Implementation is correct: cosine_distance helper computes distance correctly (0 for identical, ~1 for orthogonal), find_similar_memory is repository-scoped and skips self for updates, store() rejects entries with distance < 0.15. Eight test cases verify all edge cases. No blocking issues found.
